title: M. Murat Ardag, Ph.D.
text:
I’m a practitioner with expertise across a broad set of methods and techniques, including machine learning, deep learning, language models, and time series analysis. I am also well-versed in statistical inference, with a strong foundation in the general structural equation modeling framework1. My statistical toolkit extends to psychometric networks2, various network modeling applications, and survival analysis3.
I bring expertise to various domains, including political consulting, targeted communication, and public administration. My track record includes work on consumer behavior, customer segmentation, institutional culture, and mental well-being.

title: M. Murat Ardag, Ph.D.
text:
Footnotes


This has been the workhorse of psychometrics for decades, with extended applications in causal inference. It is a flexible modelling approach for concepts that are not directly measurable.↩︎
This has emerged as an alternative or supplementary method to structural equation modeling.↩︎
If you have a marketing background, read this as customer lifetime value or churn; sociologists call this time-to-event analysis or event history analysis. Whatever you call it, this is used to evaluate whether and when an event occurs.↩︎
